entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - RFC Destination on OAuth2
entry.content:
In this situation, SAP S4 works as a client which consumes a service exposed by other service provider system.

Prerequisite

Before configruation, you will get a service key from the service provider including a client id/client secret/token url similar like this

{
  "private_key": "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\nMIIEvQIBADANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQEFAASCBKcwggSjAgEAAoIBAQDJ... (actual private key) ...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n", 
  "client_id": "123456789012345678901", 
  "token_uri": "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token" 
}

Configuration

1. Create a Client Profile

TCODE: SE80

for instance, if the client is to consume a Mulesoft API, we can name it ZMULE as below

2. Configure Profile

TCODE: SOAUTH2_CLIENT

click create button

Select the client profile created just now and input a client id from the service key you got from the service provider.

in our example, the client id in the Prequisite section is '123456789012345678901'.

then fill in the token url

in the next tab fill in the client secret

save the configuration.

3. Unit Test

The easist way to conduct a connectivity test is via RFC Destination

TCODE: SM59

Get a working endpoint from the service provider and fill the URL into the RFC destination with type G

In the Logon & Security tab, select the client profile configured in the last step.

in the security section, activate SSL and select the view containing the trusted server certificate of service provider system.

click on button 'Connection Test'

you will get a view like this showing that authentication is working. On the other hand, you need to check in the service provider system if any incoming traffic.

Once the RFC destiantion is ready, you can use it as a building block in 

  • Intergation server configuration in SXMB_ADM, or
  • Service Consumer configuration in SOAMANAGER manually, or
  • HTTP_CLIENT in ABAP, etc

 

However, By hard coding the URL, ABAP can still implement an http client consuming a service, similar like this

DATA: lo_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
      lv_url         TYPE string VALUE 'https://example.com',
      lv_response    TYPE string.

cl_http_client=>create_by_url(
  EXPORTING
    url                = lv_url
  IMPORTING
    client             = lo_http_client
  EXCEPTIONS
    argument_not_found = 1
    plugin_not_active  = 2
    internal_error     = 3
    OTHERS             = 4 ).

However this is not a recommended way. The connection information is better to be configurable so that when the content has been transported into susequential systems like QA, Pre-Prod or Prod, it would be more flexible to update

entry.title: Re: AI capabilities for custom Application
entry.content:
@Abinaya05  ... @meenakshignanakannan  view is really good and clear and I like it.

My suggest is :  

To answer your Q, yes, the scenario is possible, but with one important clarification:

Joule itself cannot be embedded directly inside a custom Fiori or RAP app as on today today, hopefully one day and again that's best of my understand , if someone have better understanding pls feel free to correct.

What is possible "Is to add GenAI capabilities around your app using SAP AI Core, SAP AI Services, or an LLM hosted on BTP. Your app provides the business data (via OData), and the AI layer provides the natural‑language insights.

For your use case (AI answering questions about the list report):

Expose the report data through your OData service

Build a small BTP service that performs grounding/RAG on that data

Call your AI service from the UI5 app and return the answer to the user

If you are building a RAP‑based app, the integration is even cleaner because RAP gives you structured APIs and better extensibility for AI scenarios.

So in short:
Lift & Shift → Yes, possible
RAP App → Even better for AI integration
Direct Joule embedding → Not supported yet, but custom GenAI integration is fully achievable  

Feel free to respond if this help.. Happy learning.

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
SAP ECC 6.0 – Fiscal Year Variant Change ( 5-4-4 → Calendar Month)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently reviewing a Fiscal Year Variant (FYV) change in SAP ECC 6.0 and would appreciate guidance from who have handled similar scenarios.

Current Situation

  • Financial year: 1 July → 30 June
  • Existing FY variant is year‑dependent
  • Period 12 ends on 26 June, not 30 June (as per 5-4-4 method)
  • Dates 27–30 June are posted to next financial year (Period 1)
  • This early‑close model was historically implemented (likely for operational / RE‑FX reasons)
  • The setup is used across FI, Asset Accounting, MM, SD, RE‑FX, CO, BW, payroll posting, and custom jobs

New Business Requirement

Business is planning an SAP ECC → SAP S/4HANA conversion. Therefore, business want to align its fiscal calendar to calendar month end periods prior to S/4HANA migration. (from 5-4-4 to Calendar month)

(27–30 June 2026 must remain in the current FY, not roll into the next one)

From our understanding, this cannot be achieved without changing fiscal period boundaries in OB29.

Questions to the Community

  1. In SAP ECC 6.0, how risky is it in practice to modify an existing year‑dependent FY variant with historical postings?
  2. Has anyone successfully modified an existing FY variant (instead of creating a new one) without later issues?
  3. Are there any SAP‑supported alternatives to include 27–30 June in the same FY without redefining the FYV?
  4. From an audit/governance perspective, which approach have you found easier to defend?
  5. Any specific Asset Accounting, BW or RE‑FX pitfalls we should be aware of?

We are looking for real‑world ECC experience, not just OB29 theory.

Any guidance, lessons learned, or relevant SAP Notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - RFC Destination on Client Certificate
entry.content:
In this case, SAP S/4 HANA works as a client. It consumes service from provider system. 

Prerequisite

install openssl

here are the steps for configuration

1. Generate Private key by openssl

openssl genrsa -out private.key 2048

2. Generate a certificate by openssl

openssl req -new -x509 -key private.key -out client.crt -days 365

here is an example. The custom value has been highlighted.

  • Country Name (2 letter code) [AU]:AU
  • State or Province Name (full name) [Some-State]:Victoria
  • Locality Name (eg, city) []:Melbourne
  • Organization Name (eg, company) [Internet Widgits Pty Ltd]:ServiceProvider
  • Organizational Unit Name (eg, section) []:IT
  • Common Name (e.g. server FQDN or YOUR name) []:TEST
  • Email Address []:

3. Merge key and certificate into .pfx by openssl

Since SAP doesn't support import private key directly, we can only merge the key file and certificate file into a .pfx file , then import the .pfx file to SAP S4.

openssl pkcs12 -export -out client.pfx -inkey private.key -in client.crt

 4. Upload .pfx into a new PSE in S4

TCODE: STRUST

you maybe need to create a new PSE at first

create a new SSL client identity

import the .pfx file

input the password for accessing the file

import certificate.

after the .pfx file has been imported, double click on the PSE, double click on the subject on the right, the certificate information will be shown in the view below. click the button 'add to cert. list'. The certificate will be placed in the list.

5. Unit Test

since SAP S4 is working as a client, we will create a RFC Destination type G to check the connection.

TCODE: SM59

Create a RFC destination with type G. Fill in the service URL information.

in the Logon & Security tab, check Active option for SSL and in the dropdown menu of SSL Client PSE ID, choose the one created just now.

save the RFC destination and conduct a connection test.

you maybe get an error response like this. as long as the information is coming from the service provider system, it means the authentication is working.

Once the RFC Destination has been configured, it can be used as a building block in many places like:

  • Service Consumer in  SOAMANAGER
  • Integration Engine for middleware like CPI

Conclusion

RFC Destination does support client certificate

entry.title: SAP FI Asset accounting treatment with CO WBS AUC
entry.content:
Hello

We are in a situation where we need to review possible approaches under the assumption that SAP CO WBS must be used.


[Assumption]
- WBS is mandatory for all entities, including the U.S. entity
- All capital investment-related costs must pass through WBS

────────────────────────────────

[Option 1: Standard (Full PS Process)]

Advance Payment (F-48 + WBS)
→ WBS Actual Cost Collection
→ Month-end Settlement (CJ88)
→ AuC Creation/Settlement
→ Final Asset Capitalization

- Characteristics
· WBS is required from the advance payment stage
· All costs are accumulated on WBS before settlement
· Asset capitalization is processed via AuC

- Pros
· Full traceability of investment flow
· Enables project-level cost and budget control
· Fully aligned with SAP standard process

- Cons
· Higher operational complexity
· More processing steps required

────────────────────────────────

[Option 2: Lightweight (Early Settlement)]

Advance Payment (F-48 + WBS)
→ WBS Actual Cost Collection
→ Immediate or Short-term Settlement
→ AuC
→ Early Final Asset Capitalization

- Characteristics
· WBS usage is mandatory (policy compliance)
· Minimized WBS holding period
· Allows interim or early settlement

- Pros
· Reduced operational burden
· Faster asset capitalization
· Better fit for U.S. entity operations

- Cons
· Shorter WBS-based analysis period
· Reduced depth in project-level analysis

────────────────────────────────

[Common Principle (Critical)]

- Direct asset posting with WBS ❌
- The following flow must be followed:

Cost Occurrence → WBS → Settlement → AuC → Final Asset

────────────────────────────────

[Key Difference]

Option 1 (Standard)
→ WBS-driven management (Project Driven)

Option 2 (Lightweight)
→ Asset-driven management (WBS is a mandatory pass-through)

If there are any errors or potential issues with the proposed approach, please feel free to share your feedback.

entry.title: How to automatically release Outbound Delivery upon Sales Order release (for specific Site and Custo
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to achieve the following requirement in SAP Business ByDesign and need your guidance on the best approach.

**Requirement:**
- **Trigger:** When Sales Order is released
- **Action:** Automatically release Outbound Delivery (with Goods Issue posted)
- **Scope:** For a specific Site and Customer Account combination only

**Questions:**

1. **Is it possible to achieve the above requirement using only standard configuration?**
- If YES, what configuration settings are required?

2. **I have investigated Logistics Model settings and understood as follows. Is my understanding correct?**

**Pattern 1: Standard Shipping WITH tasks**
- Flow: Sales Order → Warehouse Task Creation → Task Confirmation → Outbound Delivery Creation → (Auto Release possible) → Goods Issue
- **Release Outbound Delivery = Automatically**: ✅ Can be configured in Logistics Model
- Trigger: Outbound Delivery is automatically created and released when warehouse task is confirmed
- Reference: KBA 1872028

**Pattern 2: Standard Shipping WITHOUT tasks (PGI method)**
- Flow: Sales Order → PGI execution → Outbound Delivery creation + Goods Issue simultaneously
- **Release Outbound Delivery = Automatically**: ❌ Cannot be configured in Logistics Model
- Error: "You cannot automatically release an outbound delivery if the logistics model does not contain a task"
- Reference: KBA 3503755

3. **If standard configuration cannot achieve this requirement, what are the recommended approaches?**

**Note:** Please respond following the question numbering above for clarity.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAMANAGER Manual Configuration on Oauth2
entry.content:
In this case, SAP S4 HANA works as a client (service consumer) . The service provider does support OAuth2 authentication.

Prerequisite

an OAuth2 Client Profile should be configured. Please refer to here for detail steps.

Configuration in SOAMANAGER

TCODE: SOAMANAGER

click on the 'web service configuration'

input an outbound interface, say SalesOrderNotification_Out

Create a logical port for this outbound interface. in the step 2, consumer security, select OAuth2.0 and choose the client profile configured just now

The rest of configuration is the same as the steps for normal web service configuration.

entry.title: Re: Multi-Bank Connectivity integration with ABN-Amro ban...
entry.content:
According to the SAP Help Portal documentation, SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity (MBC) can connect to any bank, regardless of whether SAP has previously worked with that bank or whether the bank is a member of the SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity network.

The documentation states that:

“In general, SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity can connect to any bank, regardless if SAP has worked with a specific bank before or not.”

When a bank is not a member bank, the integration is implemented using one of the standard connectivity options supported by SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity, which must be aligned with the bank during onboarding. The supported options are:

  • SFTP (Host‑to‑Host)
  • EBICS
  • SWIFT

The documentation also clarifies that:

  • The member bank channel is recommended only when the bank is a member, but it is not mandatory
  • Customers can use a mix of different connection types within the same SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity setup

From a process perspective:

  • The customer connects S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity
  • The SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity onboarding team supports the setup of the bank connection once the connectivity type is agreed
  • Integration follows SAP’s standard TEST → PROD onboarding process, including end‑to‑end testing

Relevant SAP documentation:

  • SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity to Bank / FSI
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/sap-multi-bank-connectivity-to-bank-fsi
  • SFTP (H2H), EBICS, Member Bank – Test Integration
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/sftp-ebics-member-bank-test-integration
  • SWIFT – Test Integration
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/swift-test-integration

Summary:
The absence of ABN‑Amro as a member bank does not block integration. The bank can be onboarded using SAP‑standard connectivity options following the documented SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity onboarding procedures.

entry.title: Re: Fix delivery dates for production in Sales Scheduling...
entry.content:
In SAP Sales Scheduling Agreements, the “frozen zone” is controlled by customizing settings in Materials Management and Production Planning, not by the production go‑ahead period. To ensure schedule lines within the frozen horizon cannot be changed, you need to configure the firming horizon in MRP and align it with the SSA release logic.

Firming Horizon in MRP defines the period during which planned orders and schedule lines are “firmed” and protected from automatic changes. Within this horizon, MRP will not reschedule or delete existing lines, ensuring stability for production and procurement.

In IMG > Material Requirements Planning > Planning > Define the Maximum MRP Period. Here, defines the period during which planned orders and schedule lines are “firmed” and protected from automatic changes. Within this horizon, MRP will not reschedule or delete existing lines, ensuring stability for production and procurement. Similarly, on the sales side, configure Release Profile Horizons. Ensure the release horizon matches or exceeds the MRP firming horizon.

entry.title: Re: Restrictions by company code and Plant in SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello @Beatriz96,

from the screenshots provided, like the one below it seems to me that you mainly focused on the restrictions of type Write, Read.

However, the visibility is controlled by the restriction types labeled Read, Value Help / or only Read.

So if you maintain these fields according to your authorization concept, visibility should be more limited.

You can find some more info here: How to Define Authorizations Based on Restrictions | SAP Help Portal

 

Another tip: To understand which restrictions are granting access to certain values you can use the App Display Authorization Trace.

  • In your case, you would need to start a trace for a user that has role BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010 assigned (and ideally only this role). 
  • Then this user needs to log on to the system and open Manage Purchase orders app F0842A and display all purchase orders.
  • Afterwards, in App Display Authorization Trace you can deactivate the trace and then analyze the provided trace data.
  • Since the visibility is too high, you want to analyze the results that appear for "Access Category: Read" and "Authorization Check Status: Successful".
  • Here you can see which restriction granted access. You can then use this info to maintain the specific restriction in Maintain Business Roles.
  • More info on the Authorization Trace can be found here: Display Authorization Trace | SAP Help Portal.

 

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: gATP determine delivery/GI date based on shipping poi...
entry.content: Standard APO gATP does not use the shipping point calendar for delivery/GI dates. If you need Saturday shipments only for one shipping point, you will need either a custom enhancement or a workaround in SD delivery scheduling to incorporate the shipping point calendar into the date determination logic

entry.title: Re: Delivery Address absent in Purchase Requsition for an...
entry.content: The difference is driven by whether the requisition line is for a stock material (with master data) or a non‑stock/free‑text item. Stock materials rely on plant/storage location from the material master, so no delivery address is populated. Non‑stock items require an explicit delivery address, so the field is filled.

entry.title: Notification alerts for an export dispatch
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We have the reqeuirements from the client as follows: 

Rqeusting you to provide me the exact configuration guidance to map the scenario in SAP Public Cloud 2602.

1. When an order is received for export sales, a notification should be sent to the RM/PM Department.

2. Upon receiving the alert, the department will check the availability of RM/PM.

3. After the availability check, a notification—whether RM/PM is available or not—should be sent to the MD and other stakeholders.

4. If RM/PM is available, a notification should then be sent to the Production team to initiate manufacturing.

5. Once the product is ready, a notification should be sent to the MD, stakeholders, and the Dispatch team.

6. After billing is completed, a final notification should be sent to all stakeholders, including the MD.

7. The workflow should also include process tracking, highlighting any delays and specifying the number of delayed days.

 

Regards,

Tushar Sonawane

entry.title: Re: Plant creation design with S4 and Ariba as Indirect P...
entry.content: Create plants only for real manufacturing sites and any location that physically ships or receives stock. Do not create plants for corporate offices that only handle indirect procurement, services, or salaries. Use company codes, cost centers, and Ariba integration instead. For corporate offices that sell products but don’t hold inventory, you can manage them via sales org/distribution channel without defining a plant. This way, you avoid unnecessary complexity in your S/4 landscape while still covering all logistics and procurement needs.

entry.title: Taxes could not be determined correctly

entry.title: Re: Exclude condition types from Profit Margin Calculation
entry.content:
Since these surcharge conditions are not statistical, they will be included in margin by default. To exclude them, you need to adjust their margin relevance,  either by condition purpose settings if available in Public Cloud, or by using extension logic to override PCIP calculation for those condition types. 

Also, for this type of query, please see that you share the relevant screen shots for better understanding.  It is not clear how your current pricing procedure is configured

entry.title: Re: 2ET: Correct CBC Org Structure for Sales Org / Plant...
entry.content:
Even though DE01 is visible in Organizational Structure, the CBC assignment won’t appear unless the sales area (sales org + distribution channel + division) is fully defined and linked to a plant.

If DE01 is missing in “Assign Sales Organization – Distribution Channel – Plant,” it means one of those links (usually plant-to-sales org or sales area definition) is incomplete.

Reference example (Arc Reactor tenant):

  • Sales Org GE01 → Company Code GE01
  • Sales Area GE01/10/00 fully defined
  • Plant GE01 linked to Sales Org GE01 and Distribution Channel 10
  • Plant GE01 connected to shipping point and storage location
  • CBC assignment visible and functional

So yes, if all objects are visible but the CBC assignment is missing, that clearly indicates an incomplete hierarchy. The fix is to rebuild or correct the missing links in CBC so the system generates the assignment.

entry.title: Re: Interest rate does not match interest postings
entry.content:
Good day @dlwsupport 

Thank you for your question.

For your contract, the system is not using a simple monthly interest formula such as payment x annual rate/12.

The lease valuation is based on compound interest on the opening lease liability for each interval. So, the interest calculation method affects the amount you see, and the repayment amount is calculated as repayment= clearing minus interest.

The formula to trace the interest amount is:

Interest= Opening lease liability x (1+ annualinterestrate) (days/basedays)-1

Then, Repayment= Payment amount-interest, Closing liability= Opening Liability-Repayment

If your contract is using a 30-day month/360 days year basis, then at 3% annual interest the effective monthly factor is: (1+0.03) ^ (30/360)-1 = 0.246626977% (approx. 0.247%)

That means the system calculates interest on the opening liability balance, not directly on the payment amount. For example, if you treat your contract as 12 monthly payments of 1,000 in areas, the opening lease liability would be about 11,809.92, and the first month interest would be about 29.13.

So, the mismatch you see usually happens because of one or more of these reasons:

  • Simple interest was used instead of compound interest,
  • The day count basis was assumed incorrectly,
  • The payment form is different from the assumption,
  • An addition contract condition is or is not valuation relevant.

It is important to note that the KBA 3504599 link you forwarded can be confusing because that link is for SAP Business ByDesign, not for S4H Public Cloud, so, i would not recommend using it as the main product reference for your case.

Here are references for more guidance

2699475 - Interest Calculation Method in Real Estate Leasing Valuation - IFRS16 - SAP for Me

3565481 - Valuation cash flow: Calculation of the repayment amount - SAP for Me

3653143 - Central KBA: Interest Calculation in RE-FX - SAP for Me

3531206 - RE-FX: RECEPR000, RECEPR116, RECEPR151, RECEPR502, RECEPR503, RECEPR506 when the Present Value Specified is used - SAP for Me

 Here is a quick check you can carry out.

  • In your Contract Management app, check the valuation parameters (interest rate, payment form, contract dates, etc).

  • Check the conditions such as monthly rent condition, due date rule, any one-time condition and whether it is statistical.

  • In the condition tab, check the valuation simulation and compare the first period using the formula mentioned earlier.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: TPM10 is generating '9000 flows' for deposits
entry.content:
Hi Marina,

Regarding Interest Capitalization: please note that this is a position change rather than a cash settlement, so there are no incoming or outgoing payments. We need to update the posting specifications to reflect this as an internal adjustment instead of a payment flow. Position posting in position currency.

entry.title: Re: Blend Data in SAP Analytics Cloud Stories
entry.content:
Hi @michael_melters,

thank you for your detailed response.
Unfortunately, I am unable to find an option to activate the new table experience. As shown in the screenshot, the table widget displays the message “Blending is not supported in optimized design mode.”

We are currently using Story Reporting without SAC. We do not have SAP Analytics Cloud; we only use the embedded Story Reporting within SuccessFactors.

In the classic (old) design mode, data blending in a table widget was possible. However, when using this mode, the system now displays the message:
“Classic Stories are being phased out; while you can still create and edit using this experience, within the next few QRC updates you will only be able to create new experiences using the optimized design.”

This puts us in a difficult position, as data blending in table widgets was a key requirement for us.
Could you please advise how we can resolve this in the future?

Thank you very much for your help.

Best Regards
Petra

entry.title: Does Sap datasphere supports transferring wirting outbound data to snowflake table
entry.content:
We are evaluating SAP Datasphere / SAP Business Data Cloud capabilities for data integration with Snowflake.

From the SAP documentation, we understand that Snowflake is supported in SAP Datasphere via Open Connectors. However, the documentation appears to describe Open Connectors primarily as inbound data sources into Datasphere, i.e., for reading or ingesting data.

Clarification requested

Can SAP Datasphere natively support writing or loading data into Snowflake tables, for example:

  • Persisting data from Datasphere into Snowflake tables
  • Supporting batch or scheduled loads
  • Acting as an outbound integration target for Snowflake

If this is supported:

  • What SAP‑recommended mechanism enables this?

If this is not supported:

  • Is Datasphere intended to be consumption‑only with respect to Snowflake, with outbound persistence handled by other SAP services?

We are looking for product‑level clarification on SAP Datasphere capabilities (not solution design or consulting guidance).

Any official clarification or experience‑based insights would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Building a CAP App with Fiori Integration – Part 1: Environment & HANA Cloud Setup
entry.content:
In this blog series we are going to see how to create a Basic CAPM Application from scratch through BTP Trial Account and Integrate it with Fiori. 

--> The First Part of this series will explain all the Pre-Requisites to be done before creating CAP Application. 

--> Second Part of this series will explain how to create CAP Application and Integrate it with Fiori.

Cloud Based Applications may look complex from outside but when blocks are perfectly aligned even a beginner can build a powerful CAP application using BAS. Let’s dive in and start arranging those blocks from the ground up.

Tools Required: BAS, HANA Cloud Central

At end of this series we can able to successfully run a CAP Application in Fiori Generated App and able to create entries in HANA Cloud Central based DB from the Fiori App. 

1.  Create a BTP Trial Account/Use existing Global Account for the Development Process. Access to your SubAccount as below from Account Explorer -> SubAccounts. 

2. Now get into Sub Account and Navigate to Instances and Subscriptions as below and then click Create Button. 

3. Under Services tab -> Select SAP HANA Cloud & in Plan select Tools/hana-cloud which makes service compatible for shareable instance. 

Note: If SAP HANA Cloud is not showing under services tab then add it from Entitlements->Add service Plans.

4. After Creation, the SAP HANA Cloud Central tool will be added to subscription as below along with Business Application Studio. Note: Incase if BAS is not added then add that as well from Create Button. 

5. Once the HANA Cloud and BAS set up is done, then navigate to Security->Users and assign these Roles.

Roles Required :

Note: These roles should be assigned to the user and with that HANA Cloud can be accessed.

Before moving into next point lets have a quick overview on what is SAP HANA Cloud Central and BAS. 

SAP HANA Cloud Central - It manages SAP HANA Cloud instances within the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It acts as a centralized dashboard to create, monitor, and maintain both SAP HANA database instances and Data Lake instances.

SAP BAS - SAP Business Application Studio is a cloud-based Integrated Development Environment (IDE) designed specifically to build and extend SAP applications.

Basically SAP HANA Cloud Central helps to manage data through cloud instances and BAS helps in building data. We can decide how the data should look/maintained from BAS.  

Now let's continue with the Pre-Requisites;

6. As a next step, navigate to Cloud Foundry->Spaces and ensure if space dev is available. If that is not there then create dev space using Create space Button. This Space is really important as it acts as a deployment target from CAP where code is deployed and executed.

7. Navigate to HANA Cloud and click on Create Instance. Again, Creation of HANA Cloud Instance is as important as creation of dev space in cloud foundruy. It acts as the database-as-a-service (DBaaS) where application’s data is permanently stored. HANA Cloud instance also contains HDI Containers which act as a bridge between design-time code from BAS into run-time database objects like tables and views.

8. On Type section select Instance configuration as Configure Manually ( Select other options if you want to configure instance in different way ) and Instance type as SAP HANA Database as we are creating Instance for HANA DB. If Data Lake is expected then select Data lake instead of HANA DB. 

9. In section SAP HANA Database General, Cloud foundry tab to be navigated for filling instance related details as the deployment happens for Cloud Foundry. But it is disabled by default. Henceforth user should select Sign in to cloud Foundry Environment and after sign in the Cloud Foundry tab will be enabled. In that tab fill the details as below. 

10. Post filling the important section as above, navigate to other sections where Connection Details and Advanced settings needs to be mentioned. For a Minimalistic App execution these tabs are not required and henceforth I've skipped it. It needs to be filled based on Business expectations. 

11. Enable Data lake if required and click on create instance as below. 

12. Once above process is done, Test Instance will be created for SAP HANA Cloud central as below and it acts as DB for the Developments made from CAP Project. 

13. Now SAP HANA Cloud is all set, Let's Navigate to BAS and create Dev Space. Get back to your subaccount. Navigate Instances and Subscriptions -> Business Application Studio. Click on create Dev space.

14. Define Dev space name and select Full Stack cloud Application option as below. 

15. With that Dev space will be created in BAS. 

From here, let's continue on Part: 2 where full stack CAP Application will be built and Deployed/Integrated with SAP HANA Cloud. 

What we did till now.

-> Created Trial Account

-> Enabled necessary subscriptions and Roles 

-> Set up SAP HANA Cloud Central

-> Created Instance and ensured Dev space is available

-> Set up BAS Dev space. 

PART: 2 Link - Stay Tuned...

entry.title: What's New in SAP BTP - Q1 2026 Innobytes
entry.content:
This first quarter has set the tone for 2026, bringing in new ideas and innovations all around Data, Joule and more. Let’s dive into some of the highlights Q1 has brought us.

 

 

AI & Data Foundation Highlight

Discovery and Data Agent in SAP HANA Cloud

The new Discovery and Data Agents are lowering learning barriers for developers and data users who just need answers. These new agents make it easier to explore, analyze, and act on data without needing deep knowledge of the underlying artifacts.

To see this demo in action, watch the video (starting at 0:11).

Learn more

  • Discovery & Data Agent on SAP Community
  • Register for the SAP HANA Cloud Data & Discovery Agents - Beta Program

 

Integration Highlight

Fresh Connections in SAP Integration Suite

New adapters for SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP help transform your integration landscape, breaking down data silos, streamlining operations, and allow rapid adoption of new capabilities to innovate faster. 

The latest release offers:

  • The BambooHR adapter enables receiving and processing employee lifecycle data effectively.
  • The Microsoft Outlook Sender and Receiver adapter allows seamless integration for calendars, mail, attachments, and messaging directly into your mission-critical workflows. 

For Edge Integration Cella:

  • The File adapter, enabling secure file exchange with NFS systems
  • The OFTP2 adapter for compliant B2B connections to securely exchange messages from business partners. 

See the new integration content (starting at 2:14).

Learn more:

  • Integration Adapter
  • SAP Integration Suite on SAP Community

 

Agent & Application Development Highlights

API based Action Error Handling

In real-world automation, failures are inevitable. What matters is how the system handles them. This innovation brings precision and predictability to API error handling in SAP Build Process Automation on SAP BTP. When configuring an API-based Action step, enable error handling and define specific errors, handling authentication failures precisely. You can create generic categories, such as 4XX or 5XX, handling broader client or server issues.

The inability to define category overlap eliminates ambiguity, and deterministic runtime behavior is guaranteed. When an error occurs, precedence is strictly enforced:

  • Specific errors first
  • Generic category second
  • Undefined fallback last

At the Action level, you can configure retries that define the number of attempts and the delay between them. Afterwards, the error handling logic is executed. Every stage is fully logged in Monitoring, ensuring complete traceability and turning error handling from a black box into a controlled, observable workflow decision.

Watch the demo (starting at 3:35).

 

Conversational Work in SAP Build Work Zone 

There is now a powerful new way to work in SAP Build Work Zone. Save time by simply asking Joule what you need instead of clicking through multiple pages.

In the demo (starting at 5:33), a new project workspace for a cross-functional initiative is set up through a short conversation with Joule, enabling easy navigation, no technical steps, and only one screen. SAP Build Work Zone becomes more conversational, more intuitive, and more aligned with how people actually work.

Learn more:

  • Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

 

Core Platform Highlight

SAP Automation Pilot and Joule: Unlocking AI-Powered Ops Agents

The SAP Automation Pilot and Joule turn repeatable operational tasks into intelligent, agent-driven capabilities on SAP BTP.

By starting with a real operational process like a S/4HANA health check, use Automation Pilot’s low-code/no-code interface. Here, the necessary steps, inputs and validations can be collected and grouped into a Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server. The MCP Server exposes these automations as governed, reusable tools that can be safely consumed by AI agents without writing custom APIs.

Once published, connect it to Joule Studio to build a Joule Ops Agent that understands when and how to invoke these tools. The Ops Agent can now detect issues or fetch system status, triggering the right automation behind the scenes.

The DevOps or Admin team can simply ask the Ops Agent in Joule to ‘check my HANA Cloud and App instances health and remediate common issues’ by using the MCP Server to execute these steps securely, consistently, and audibly.

For a demo, watch the video (starting at 7:35).

Learn more:

  • Blog Post: From Automation to Agentic Ops
  • Blog Post: Meet your Ops Agents

 

Share your thoughts 

These videos are for YOU, and we would love to hear your impressions. How do you like the video? How can we improve upcoming videos? Please share your feedback by liking this post or leaving a comment below.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

You Might Also Be Interested In

  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • Unlocking SAP BTP: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP.

entry.title: Re: TM - Public cloud - Location geocoordinates
entry.content:
Good day @jignesh_parikh 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, in the scenario you described you can maintain latitude and longitude manually for locations. If you consider doing it manually, that can be done in the Manage Locations app, and you do not require the HANA spatial services. When you manually maintain, the Calculated Geocoordinates function is disabled.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/1cdd4e9bd51d468a97978df6b3a9a00c.html?locale=en-US

3709474 - Unable to Find Airports or Ports and Create new Locations in Manage Locations app - SAP for Me

 

So, in the Manage Locations app,

use the Geocoordinates tab to maintain latitude and longitude manually. Then save the location.

 

If your requirement is only to have geocoordinates available so that distance calculation can work with the location master data you maintained, then this manual maintenance is the first step to carry out.

However, if your customer wants automated geocoding, routing, or provider-based map and distances services, then HANA spatial services is the relevant SAP BTP service.

Regarding charges and plan, see the following:

https://help.sap.com/doc/842f6fbf036d489caca610c697c9d612/Cloud/en-US/cloud_SAP_HANA_Spatial_Services_Reference_en.pdf

 

So, in summary:

Manual latitude/longitude entry is supported in Manage Location. You do not require spatial services for that.

Automated geocoding, routing, location services require HANA spatial services on BTP. This is based on your consumption.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Vendor Open Item Not Clearing
entry.content: The error means the vendor open item cannot be cleared because the asset side of the posting is missing or inconsistent. Please check the PO’s account assignment, the asset master linkage, and whether the MIRO/MIGO postings created the required asset line items. If the asset postings are missing, you may need to adjust the invoice or repost with the correct asset assignment so FI and AA are reconciled.

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP: ES Plastic Tax - Missing Tax ID for delivier...
entry.content:
If the logistic process for the EU Acquisition is Stock Transfer Import, then the tax number is taken from the sending plant. RDP has a screen in the Organization Data app, which allows maintenance of tax numbers for plants.

If the logistic process for the EU Acquisition is "Delivery as import", then from what I see, RDP takes the customer's tax number, not the sending plant's tax number.

Check which logistic process is involved - stock transfers or delivery as import.

From your explanation it seems that it is delivery as import.

Check if the customer of these deliveries has a tax number.

The Email Subject can be changes before the initial release of a document.

if you want to resubmit that document you can also change the subject.

Since only the Initial output can be resubmitted there is no feature  hardcode the last resubmitted email Subject, changes .

 

Any resubmission will take the Subject from the Initial output but it is modifiable

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi @MaximilianoDestro 

Sorry, I missed this comment until now 😞

There is no "callback" once an application job has completed, so it wouldn't be possible like that. But, in one of the comments higher up I am explaining how to have "multiple steps". So your requirement could work like this:

  1. Create a new application job template for each "other process" you need to run. For simpleness sake, let's say we have 1 and we call it "ZAPJ_START_PROCESS01"
  2. Create a new "main" application job template in the Fiori app Application Jobs. Choose the standard one that you want to run first. Then go to "Maintain Steps" and then choose ZAPJ_START_PROCESS01. Repeat the maintain steps until all your processes are done
  3. Give the new "main" application job template a name
  4. Now when your API is called, instead of starting the standard job with CL_APJ_RT_API=>SCHEDULE_JOB, you start this new "main" template. It should then execute step after step, one after the other is completed.

Hope that helps and I got your requirement right.

BR

entry.content:
Dear @tushar_sonawane53,

1. Overview / dashboard apps

  • Order to Cash Dashboard overview of key order-to-cash KPIs such as sales volume, profit margin and incoming orders
  • Sales Management Overview graphical management overview of sales data
  • My Sales Overview dashboard-style overview for day-to-day sales monitoring
  • Customer 360° View customer-centric view of related sales business documents
  • Customer Returns Overview overview of return KPIs and return trends

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/c4cce03194104a0184bd2435d9697be6.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/9afe5824a6dd4de0a80c82fbd7ad5dc2.html

2. Standard analytical reports

  • Sales Quotations Flexible Analysis standard quotation analysis for flexible business dimensions
  • Quotation Conversion Rates Valid/Not Completed quotation-to-order conversion analysis
  • Incoming Sales Orders Flexible Analysis incoming sales order trend analysis
  • Sales Volume Flexible Analysis sales volume and billing KPI trend analysis
  • Sales Volume Detailed Analysis detailed drilldown of invoiced sales by business dimensions
  • Sales Volume Check Open Sales analysis of sales volume together with open orders and open deliveries
  • Sales Volume Profit Margin analysis of sales volume versus profit margin
  • Sales Performance Plan/Actual plan-versus-actual sales analysis
  • Sales Performance Predictions predictive sales performance analysis
  • Customer Returns Flexible Analysis returns trend and return-rate analysis
  • Delivery Performance monitoring of current delivery performance for sales orders
  • Sales Order Items Backorders analysis of confirmed orders, backorders and demand fulfillment

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/e846f3b7c95a40c2b24c393f942a2855.html

3. Operational monitoring / execution apps

  • Manage Sales Quotations Version 2 operational worklist for sales quotations
  • Manage Sales Orders Version 2 central worklist for sales orders
  • Track Sales Orders end-to-end tracking of sales order fulfillment status
  • Track Sales Order Details detailed process-flow and issue view for a sales order
  • Sales Order Fulfillment Analyze and Resolve Issues monitoring and resolution of fulfillment issues such as incompletion, delivery block, billing block and credit block
  • List Incomplete Sales Documents list of incomplete sales documents requiring follow-up
  • Manage Duplicate Sales Documents identifies and manages duplicate sales documents
  • Manage Outbound Deliveries monitoring and processing of outbound deliveries
  • Manage Billing Documents monitoring and processing of invoices, credit memos, cancellations and other billing documents
  • Manage Customer Returns operational processing and monitoring of customer returns

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/a5970267d2b342fdb2f02a503aabfb38.html
